title: Memory hygiene: act on the memory-pollution passive WARN (L2 backfill / dedup as confirmed)
current_state: The passive daily digest's memory-pollution skill reports WARN (exact findings unreviewed - T-253 triages the report). Suspects per the audit: stale or contradictory L2 facts, L3 entries that should have been distilled or expired, possible L2 backfill gaps from sessions where the Groq session-end distiller failed silently. The tooling to act already exists and must be reused: memory/audit.py + memory/audit_rules.py (contradiction/conflict detection), memory/semantic_dedup.py (word-overlap dedup on L3 write), memory/salience.py (importance scoring).
target_state: Every finding in the memory-pollution report is resolved: polluted entries corrected or expired through the existing memory tools (never raw SQL against pi.db), confirmed L2 gaps backfilled via the existing distillation path, and the memory-pollution passive returns PASS. Scope is bounded by what T-253's triage confirms is real - no speculative memory refactoring.
migration_plan: 1. Read the memory-pollution report in reports/ (as triaged by T-253); enumerate concrete findings.

2. For each polluted/contradictory entry: resolve via memory_write/memory_delete or the audit tooling - keep Supabase and SQLite in agreement (the recurring write/read-divergence bug class, see solutions S-xxx for T-148/T-149/T-151, lives exactly here).
3. If L2 backfill gaps are confirmed: re-run the L1->L2 distillation (memory/pipeline.py) for the affected sessions on Groq (free tier per cost policy).
4. Rerun the memory-pollution passive - expect PASS.
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS; the memory roundtrip contract tests must stay green.
risk_notes: The write/read-divergence bug class is Pi's most recurrent (3+ solutions on record) - any fix here must go through the existing memory tool paths, never direct db edits, and must keep testing/test_memory_context_columns.py and the roundtrip contract tests green.
